name: Calais
description: Calais, son of Boreas, the North Wind, and Oreithyia, daughter of Erechtheus, King of Athens, was a winged hero in Greek mythology. He and his brother Zetes, collectively known as the Boreads, were renowned for their incredible speed and ability to fly, gifts inherited from their father. Calais is primarily known for his role in the Argonautic expedition, joining Jason and the Argonauts in their quest for the Golden Fleece. The Boreads played a crucial role in freeing Phineus, a blind king tormented by the Harpies, monstrous bird-women who stole his food. Calais and Zetes pursued the Harpies and drove them away, either killing them or forcing them to swear an oath never to bother Phineus again. Their bravery and skill were highly valued by the Argonauts, contributing significantly to the success of their voyage. Calais's legacy is tied to his heroic deeds, particularly his assistance to Phineus and his participation in the legendary Argonaut expedition, solidifying his place as a notable figure in Greek mythology. He is often depicted as a strong, swift, and courageous warrior, embodying the power of his father, the North Wind. The Boreads represent the untamed forces of nature and their potential for both destruction and aid. His lineage connects him to both the divine and mortal realms, highlighting the complex interactions between gods, heroes, and humans in Greek myth. His death is said to have occurred while trying to protect the Argonauts from an attack.
subtitle: Winged son of the North Wind

enemies.name: Various mythical creatures encountered on the Argonaut's journey

keyMyths.title: The Harpies and Phineus
keyMyths.description: Calais and his brother Zetes chased away the Harpies who were tormenting the blind king Phineus, allowing him to eat in peace. Their intervention was crucial for Phineus to provide the Argonauts with valuable information.

keyMyths.title: Joining the Argonauts
keyMyths.description: Calais and Zetes joined Jason and the Argonauts on their quest for the Golden Fleece. Their unique abilities, particularly their speed and flight, proved invaluable to the crew.

keyMyths.title: Death of Calais
keyMyths.description: Accounts vary, but some say Calais died while protecting the Argonauts, showcasing his bravery and dedication to his companions. This highlights his heroic nature and sacrifice.

sources.author: Apollonius Rhodius
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Calais and the Argonauts.
